opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: My hands and feet feel like they are submerged in boiling oil, and I get so dizzy I can't walk. It has been getting worse and worse all day and now I can't bear anything touching my skin — even the bedsheet is agony. When I try to stand up the room spins and I feel like I'm going to pass out.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: The burning has flared up to the worst it's ever been over the last day and I'm so dizzy standing up that I can't get around safely on my own tonight — I don't know if I need to go to hospital.

symptom_narrative.onset: the burning has been building up over the past day or so and has flared to the worst it's ever been tonight; the dizziness on standing has been bad at the same time
symptom_narrative.duration: this severe flare is about 24 hours in; the underlying burning nerve pain has been there for a long time
symptom_narrative.character: a constant deep burning, like my hands and feet are in boiling oil; the skin is so sensitive that clothing or a sheet touching it is agony
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the worst it has ever been — completely unbearable and I can't be touched
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: both hands and both feet equally, and it feels like it's creeping further up my arms and legs
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant, no let-up; standing up brings on the dizziness and near-fainting
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing — the strongest over-the-counter painkillers did absolutely nothing
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: anything touching the skin, and standing up makes the dizziness worse
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: severe dizziness and feeling like I'll faint when I stand, going pale and sweaty, and abnormal sweating; no fever, no chest pain, no shortness of breath
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: I've had this burning nerve pain for a long time and flares before, but never this bad; a nerve test I had in the past came back normal
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I'm stuck in bed tonight because the pain is unbearable and I'm too dizzy and unsteady to stand without nearly blacking out

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: long-standing burning nerve pain in the hands and feet that a specialist has been helping me with
past_medical_history.how_managed: nerve-pain tablets and avoiding triggers; it flares from time to time

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: dizziness and near-fainting when I stand up that comes and goes
past_medical_history.when_diagnosed: part of the same nerve problem, ongoing
past_medical_history.how_managed: rising slowly and taking care when standing

current_medications_as_reported.dose_as_patient_reports: took the maximum I'm allowed today

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives alone; very frightened of falling when the dizziness hits, especially with no one else at home; there are a few things to trip over near the bed

objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: single reading on a home cuff

objective_data_offered.timing: just after standing, when the dizziness came on
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: single reading on a home cuff; had to sit straight back down

objective_data_offered.timing: checked tonight with a home thermometer

objective_data_offered.value: she is visibly distressed and flinching away from the bedsheet; her hands and feet look their normal colour, not pale, white or blue, and she says they feel warm to touch; she goes pale and sweaty when she sits herself upright
both feet and both hands feel the same as each other and feel warm; I can still move and wiggle them normally, they're just agony to touch
